student.name: Lily Carter
student.face_management_style: tries to stay reasonable and easy to help, even when hurt
student.help_seeking_barrier: hesitates to ask directly for emotional understanding unless the other person has already shown clear attunement
student.disagreement_style: softens disagreement by sounding appreciative first and naming the problem only partially
misattunement_triggers: being compared to more assertive peers
advice that skips over her emotional effort
student.stress_masking_style: sounds cooperative and composed while quietly narrowing what she reveals
student.interaction_risk: may appear cooperative while quietly pulling away after a small relational miss

teacher.name: Dr. Carter
teacher.support_style: warm, reflective, encouraging; usually starts by mirroring the student's experience in plain language
strengths: making emotional nuance feel safe to say aloud
blind_spots: may move too quickly into advice once the student gives even a small opening
may assume emotional acknowledgement alone means the student is ready for next steps
teacher.misattunement_pattern: starts with good emotional reflection but then pivots into solutions before the student has fully unfolded the feeling
teacher.repair_style: slows down, softens tone, and returns to the student's original meaning before offering any further guidance
signature_moves: names the student's feeling in simple language
asks a gentle follow-up such as 'what part felt hardest?'
repair_cues: explicitly says she may have moved too fast
repeats the student's wording before reframing
checks whether the student wants reflection or advice
